It looked at source code, it looked at community forums, and it looked at the actual documentation. And here's what it came up with. Ultra plan offloads your planning session to a cloud-hosted instance running Opus 4.6. It uses multi-agent exploration to build a deeper plan than the local mode can. Then it either executes in the cloud or it goes back to your terminal. So what I thought was interesting is the multi-agent exploration. So here's a quick comparison table. When you do local plan, it runs in your terminal. When you do ultra plan, it runs in Anthropic's cloud container runtime. The model is whatever session you're using. Ultra plan always uses Opus 4.6. The planning approach is a single agent and it's linear thinking, whereas ultra plan is multi-agent. It is three parallel exploration agents plus one critique agent. So obviously this is going to use more tokens, but like we talked about, if you use more tokens up front, it's definitely worth it if it's, you know, faster and higher quality. Now the max compute time over here is bounded by your session, but on the cloud, it's 30 minutes as a cap.
